education.program: Bachelor of Applied Science in Mechanical Engineering
awards: University of Toronto Engineering International Scholar

evidence.source_text: Developed a Python-based ETL platform that automatically extracted, validated, and normalized sales data from 3 major distributor systems into a unified report, processing thousands of transaction records across multiple reporting formats.

Engineered data transformation and validation pipelines using Pandas and NumPy to standardize SKU, promotion, and sales data, reducing manual reporting effort by approximately 40–50% and improving data consistency across business units.

Automated monthly sales reporting and Power BI dashboard refresh workflows, enabling dynamic KPI tracking, promotional analysis, and historical trend reporting through a centralized analytics dataset.

Led the development of a level 1 autonomous teleoperation last-mile delivery solution for low-speed vehicles (LSVs) using ROS 2, enabling real-time remote control, safety override, and operator-to-vehicle round-trip latency <200 ms over 5G modem.

Integrated NVIDIA Jetson Orin, STM32 microcontrollers, and multi-sensor systems including LiDAR, RTK GPS, IMU, ultrasonic sensors, and cameras supporting autonomous navigation achieving emergency stop in <150 ms, 360° environmental perception

Collaborated on integrating a real-time perception pipeline using YOLOv8 and OpenCV to detect traffic lights, pedestrians, and vehicles with >90% accuracy at 30 FPS for supporting live object detection, lane tracking, and command processing.

Built an AI-powered financial management platform that analyzed 4,000+ transactions using natural language queries, enabling users to explore spending patterns, budgets, and expense data through an interactive dashboard.

Implemented AI-driven expense reporting and recommendation features using FastAPI, MongoDB Atlas, and Gemini, Vultr generating contextual insights and approval suggestions for business users within a full-stack web application.
